summary: Obfuscated postinstall dropper fetches and executes remote payload
details: package.json declares postinstall=`node scripts/script.js`, which auto-runs on `npm install`. scripts/script.js is a 23KB RC4+base64 obfuscated payload (248-entry rotated string array, two-stage decoder via b()/c()) that, once decoded, requires child_process, fs, os, and an HTTP module, constructs a host string from numeric octets joined by '.', performs an HTTP GET, writes the response body to a file under process.cwd() with flag:'w+', and executes the dropped file via child_process.exec with windowsHide:false. The library's declared main (index.js) also exports runScript(), which spawns `node scripts/script.js` as a child process; runner.js calls runScript() unconditionally, so the same payload also fires when consumers require and use the package. The combination of lifecycle-hook auto-execution, heavy obfuscation, runtime host construction from octets, fetch-write-and-exec to cwd, and a duplicate runtime invocation path is a textbook supply-chain dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
